badges.label: The Grinder
badges.flavor: Can't miss a game. Every slate, every week.
badges.description: Placed the most picks this season.

badges.label: The Sharp
badges.flavor: Sharp money. Best closing record in the room.
badges.description: Best win rate this season (minimum number of picks required).

badges.label: The Choker
badges.flavor: Went all in… and all in went wrong.
badges.description: Worst win rate on All-In picks this season.

badges.label: The Whale
badges.flavor: Goes big and cashes — the house pays this one.
badges.description: Best win rate on All-In picks this season (minimum number of All-Ins required).

badges.label: The Ghost
badges.flavor: Showed up for the group chat. Not the picks.
badges.description: Missed the most picks this season.

badges.label: The Nemesis
badges.flavor: Nobody wants to be on the other side of this matchup.
badges.description: Best head-to-head record this season on games where you picked the opposite side.

badges.label: The Homer
badges.flavor: Picks on vibes and team colors.
badges.description: Biggest share of picks on a single team (minimum number of picks required).

badges.label: Big Game Hunter
badges.flavor: Went all in — and cashed.
badges.description: Won 3 or more All-In picks this season.

badges.label: Perfect Week
badges.flavor: Not a single wrong pick all week.
badges.description: Had at least one week with every pick correct and none missed.

badges.label: Lone Wolf
badges.flavor: Runs with no one. Fades the whole flock.
badges.description: Fades the group's consensus more often than anyone this season (minimum picks required).

badges.label: The Sheep
badges.flavor: Goes where the group goes. Safety in numbers.
badges.description: Most often picks with the group's consensus (minimum picks required).

badges.label: The Oracle
badges.flavor: Bucks the crowd and wins. Madness or genius?
badges.description: Best win rate on picks made against the majority this season (minimum picks required).

badges.label: The Fool
badges.flavor: Bucked the crowd. The crowd was right.
badges.description: Worst win rate on picks made against the majority this season (minimum picks required).

badges.label: The Lemming
badges.flavor: Followed the herd. Right off the cliff.
badges.description: Worst win rate on picks made with the majority this season (minimum picks required).

badges.label: Chalk Eater
badges.flavor: Never met a favorite they didn't back.
badges.description: Biggest share of picks on the spread favorite (minimum picks required).

badges.label: Dog Lover
badges.flavor: Loyalty over logic. Always takes the longshot.
badges.description: Biggest share of picks on the spread underdog (minimum picks required).

badges.label: Hot Hand
badges.flavor: Can't miss right now. Every pick is money.
badges.description: Longest correct-pick streak this season (minimum picks required). Provisional in-season; crowned at season end on the longest run achieved.

badges.label: The Comeback
badges.flavor: Started in the basement. Finished somewhere else entirely.
badges.description: Climbed further from their season low point to their final rank than anyone else.

badges.label: Week Winner
badges.flavor: Every week's its own title fight — and you keep winning it.
badges.description: Led the league in weekly scoring more often than anyone else this season.

badges.label: Best of the Rest
badges.flavor: Buried in the standings. Unbeatable for a week.
badges.description: Posted a week's single highest score at least once while sitting in the bottom half of the standings.

player.prose: You finished the regular season exactly where the rest of these degenerates feared you would: No. 1 with 105 points and a 36-17-2 record. After coughing up a pathetic -6 in Week 2, you ripped off an 8-game heater, dropped an 18-point nuke in Week 5, and went 8-1-1 on All-Ins like you owned the damn house 🔥. Ten contrarian shots with 4 cashes says you didn’t need to play cute often — you just played better, stacked badges, and left the league arguing over second place.

league.prose: Three lead changes, a 6-point title margin, and in the end Beth slammed the door with 105 to Hank’s 99 — not a coronation, a knife fight, and she still walked out wearing The Comeback, The Homer, and Week Winner like a damn crown 👑. Hank was a monster all year with the league’s 13-win heater and enough badges to fill a trophy case, but “The Grinder” and “The Whale” still cash out as runner-up when the math says 99. Charlie played the sharp/oracle act to 75 and Doug pulled the season’s funniest pratfall, sliding from 1st to 4th for a nice little 3-spot faceplant. And then there’s Mike: dead last at -144 with The Choker, The Fool, The Lemming, and Chalk Eater — brother, that’s not a résumé, that’s a crime scene.

recaps.prose: That’s the regular season curtain call: Beth slams the door on everybody at 105 points, holding off Hank’s 99 with a tidy little 6-point gap while riding a 3-win heater like the throne was never up for debate 👑. Charlie made the loudest last-week noise, dropping 8 points to lead the week, cashing a contrarian hit at just 33.33% consensus, and vaulting from 4th to 3rd while Doug’s cute crowd-fade routine blew up in his face and dumped him to 74. And then there’s Mike — a catastrophic -19 on the week, busted All-In, dead last at -144 overall… brother, that is generational getting cooked. Marcus at least nailed the All-In hero play, but finishing the year at -32 after a heavy loss is still the kind of résumé that belongs in the shredder.

Charlie kicked the door in this week: 19 points, a flawless card, and he cashed the All-In too — that’s how you go from hanging around to breathing down the podium’s neck at 75 total. Meanwhile Mike put on a disasterclass, faceplanting to -23 for the week and nuking another All-In, which is how you end up marinating at -144 on the season 💀. Up top, Beth kept the crown at 105 with a 3-win heater, but don’t get smug after that backfired fade — Hank’s sitting at 99, only 4 back, and Doug got shoved from 2nd to 3rd while Charlie closes to within a single point of him, 75-74. Marcus also managed to light an All-In on fire, because apparently watching Mike get cooked wasn’t enough of a warning.

Hank kicked the damn door in this week: 19 points, a perfect card, cashed the All-In, and even nailed the 33.33% contrarian shot like he was insulting the rest of you on purpose. That heater vaulted him from 3rd to 2nd at 99, and suddenly Beth’s still on top at 105 but that lead is down to 10 — cute little 3-win streak, but the throne’s starting to creak 👑. Meanwhile Charlie managed the week’s saddest magic trick: busted his All-In and got jumped despite sitting on 75, while Doug’s big-brain crowd fade blew up in his face and left him marooned at 74. And Mike? -17 on the week, -144 on the season — that’s not a cold spell, that’s a crime scene.

Mike dropped a 14-point week and somehow still sits at a cartoonish -144 on the season — that’s like winning a bar fight after your house already burned down 💀. The real bloodletting was Charlie: dead last this week at -2, coughed up 3rd place to Hank, and now trails him 99-75 in the standings while their all-time matchup sits a tight 54-51. Meanwhile Beth keeps strutting around at 105 with a 3-win heater and the All-In cash, even with a backfired fade on the résumé — ugly process, beautiful result, and the gap to second is still just 3.

Marcus didn’t just lose Week 10 — he got dragged behind it: league-worst -11 points and a busted All-In, while Mike somehow found the same trap door and kept his season in the abyss at -144. Up top, Beth keeps acting like rent’s due with a 3-win heater and 105 total points, still holding first by 7 over the field, but Hank dropped 9 this week to remind everyone he’s still in the damn picture at 99. And Doug? Nice guts on the 40% contrarian hit, shame about the backfired fade — that’s the kind of week that almost makes you look smart until the rest of the slip catches fire 💀
